title.value: Enyne diketones as substrate in asymmetric Nazarov cyclization for construction of chiral allene cyclopentenones

abstract.value: The Nazarov cyclization is one of the most powerful tools for the stereoselective synthesis of various cyclopentenone scaffolds. Therefore, developing the new classes substrate of Nazarov reaction is an important endeavor in synthetic chemistry. Herein, we report enyne diketones, enables diastereo- and enantioselective construction of chiral allene cyclopentenones in moderate to good yields with good enantioselectivities (up to 97% ee). Importantly, it is a typical example for asymmetric synthesis of cyclopentanones with allene moiety using Nazarov cyclisation. Mechanistic studies indicate that this metal-organo relay catalysis protocol involves a rhodium-catalyzed tandem oxonium ylide formation/[2,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement/reverse benzylic acid rearrangement, followed by organo-catalyzed asymmetric Nazarov cyclization/alkyne-to-allene isomerization to give the final chiral allene cyclopentenones.

warnings: Catalyst classification compresses a rhodium/organocatalyst relay system into a single allowed label; the chiral organocatalyst class is supported but not fully resolved from the provided excerpts.

value.ligand_or_chiral_source: prolinol-derived and other chiral amine catalysts
value.reaction_type: intramolecular cyclization and skeletal reorganization of allenes
